name: Monasterio de Santa Ana, Iglesia de Santiago y Castillo — circular from Jumilla
name_original: Monasterio de Santa Ana, Iglesia de Santiago y Castillo — circular desde Jumilla
region_intro: Touring cycling around Jumilla offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from plains to mountain ranges. The region is characterized by extensive vineyards, particularly of the Monastrell grape, and notable mountain ranges such as Sierra de El Carche, Sierra Larga, and Sierra de Santa Ana. These features provide varied terrain for cycling, from mostly paved paths to routes with significant elevation gains. Best touring cycling routes around Jumilla The most popular touring cycling route is Castillo de Jumilla, Iglesia de, a 24.0 miles (38.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route incorporates historic landmarks such as Castillo de Jumilla. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monasterio de Santa Ana, Iglesia de Santiago y Castillo — circular from Jumilla, a moderate 14.9 miles (24.0 km) path. It connects historical sites and offers scenic views of the surrounding area. Local touring cyclists also love the Final Stretch to the Summit loop from El Carche, a 21.1 miles (34.0 km) trail leading through Sierra de El Carche, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Jumilla is defined by mountain ranges, extensive vineyards, and varied plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Jumilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 7 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Jumilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Jumilla?
A: Jumilla offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 40 available to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to more challenging rides with significant climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Jumilla?
A: The terrain in Jumilla is quite diverse, transitioning from plains to mountain ranges. You'll encounter extensive vineyards, particularly of the Monastrell grape, and notable mountain ranges such as Sierra de El Carche, Sierra Larga, and Sierra de Santa Ana. Routes vary from mostly paved paths to those with significant elevation gains, offering varied landscapes for every cyclist.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Jumilla?
A: Yes, Jumilla has options for beginners. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are at least 2 easy touring cycling routes available, perfect for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the cycling routes in Jumilla?
A: Many routes in Jumilla incorporate historic landmarks. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017181252/castillo-de-jumilla-iglesia-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Jumilla, Iglesia de&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic Castillo de Jumilla. Another popular option, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017258596/monasterio-de-santa-ana-iglesia-de-santiago-y-castillo-circular-from-jumilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santa Ana, Iglesia de Santiago y Castillo — circular from Jumilla&lt;/a&gt;, connects historical sites like the Monasterio de Santa Ana and the Iglesia de Santiago.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or scenic viewpoints to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. Jumilla is rich in natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final Stretch to the Summit loop from El Carche&lt;/a&gt; leads through the picturesque Sierra de El Carche Regional Natural Park. You can also find routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de la Pila – Monasterio de Santa Ana loop from Jumilla&lt;/a&gt; which offers scenic views of the Sierra de la Pila.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Jumilla?
A: Jumilla offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. The region's climate is generally favorable, but spring and autumn are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures for exploring the vineyards and mountain ranges without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Jumilla?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017258596/monasterio-de-santa-ana-iglesia-de-santiago-y-castillo-circular-from-jumilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santa Ana, Iglesia de Santiago y Castillo — circular from Jumilla&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to start and end your journey in the same location while visiting key attractions.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Jumilla?
A: The touring cycling routes in Jumilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic vineyards, and the opportunities to combine active tourism with cultural and historical exploration.

Q: Are there opportunities to explore the local wine culture while cycling?
A: Definitely. Jumilla is a renowned wine region, famous for its Monastrell grape vineyards. Many cycling routes traverse these vineyards, offering a scenic journey through the rural wine country. You can explore the Jumilla Wine Route, which includes numerous wineries, allowing you to combine your ride with wine tastings and learning about the winemaking process.

Q: Are there any caves or peaks I can visit near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Jumilla features several interesting caves and mountain peaks. Notable caves include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3965352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaime el Barbudo Cave&lt;/a&gt;. For peaks, you might consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4095975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Carche Summit and Mountain Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4988220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de La Pila&lt;/a&gt;, which offer stunning views.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Jumilla?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Jumilla offers 14 difficult touring cycling routes. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and can take you through more demanding mountain terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jumilla – Monasterio de Santa Ana loop from Jumilla&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbs.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Jumilla?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the presence of easy routes suggests options suitable for families. These typically feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances, allowing for a pleasant outing with children. It's advisable to check the difficulty and length of easy routes to ensure they match your family's capabilities.
tips: The Monastery of Santa Ana, from the 16th century, is located at 680 meters above sea level and located in a privileged natural area that offers excellent views of the city and the surroundings. In addition to being a place of religious worship, it is a popular destination for hikers and bike lovers.

Jumilla Castle, located in the Region of Murcia, is an impressive medieval fortress that dominates the landscape from the top of a hill, with more than 3,500 years of history as a witness to multiple civilizations.

Declared a National Monument in 1931, it is one of the most important in the city. We will be able to differentiate several architectural styles due to the long duration of its construction. It is worth the visit and observe the altarpiece that shines inside.
